title: Research → conviction → spec → build.
description: I never start with code. I start with structured discovery — JTBD framing, talking to users, mapping the competitive landscape. Conviction comes from evidence. The spec is the artifact that proves I understand the problem before I touch the solution. By the time I write code, the hard decisions are already made.

title: Define the North Star before you define the roadmap.
description: A roadmap without a North Star metric is just a to-do list. I define the single metric that captures long-run product health, the leading indicators that predict it, and instrument the funnel to measure it before launch — not after. Metrics-informed, not metrics-driven.

title: Build yourself out of it.
description: Every system I design, I design my own removal into. Autonomous infrastructure isn't a nice-to-have — it's the exit condition. If it still needs me to run, it's not done. The agent stack running this portfolio, my social automation, and my morning brief all operate without a single daily input from me.

title: Ship in days, not sprints.
description: North went from npx create-expo-app to App Store submission in 7 days. AI systems handle scaffolding; I handle product judgment. Speed is a design choice — enabled by clear specs, decisive trade-offs, and an operating system that removes friction from execution.

title: What I kill matters as much as what I ship.
description: The fastest way to slow a product down is to ship everything. Every feature not built is scope that doesn't need to be maintained, debugged, or explained to users. I make explicit kill decisions and document them. The discipline of the kill list is what keeps the product sharp.
